LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CHALEUR, LLC AND PAUL CHARLTON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION UNDER THE SF CHARTER. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE TRESPASSED UPON AND MISUSED PIER 38 ARE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THE CLAIMS. THE EXISTENCE OF THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION IS NOT A BASIS TO DISMISS OR ABATE THIS ACTION. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT UNCERTAIN AND DEFENDANTS CAN REASONABLY RESPOND TO THE ALLEGATIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E. A. KARNOW, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED..

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO PAUL CHARLTON AND CHALEUR, LLC'S CROSS CLAIMS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO ALL CLAIMS AND CROSS-CLAIMS BETWEEN THE CITY AND DEFENDANT CURTIS LIND IS GRANTED. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT EXISTS FOR ANY CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT LIND DOES NOT DISPUTE ANY OF THE FACTS RECITED IN PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF DISPUTED FACTS. PLAINTIFF HAS PREVAILED ON THE TRESPASS AND NUISANCE CLAIMS AS A MATTER OF LAW. DEFENDANT DOES NOT HAVE A VALID SUBLEASE FOR ANY PORTION OF THE PROPERTY AT PIER 38. DEFENDANT IS INTERFERING WITH PLAINTIFF'S FREE USE AND ENJOYMENT OF THE PROPERTY, WHICH CONSTITUTES A NUISANCE UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 3479. DEFENDANT'S CROSS-CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE, AND NEGLIGENT INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IN CALIFORNIA GOVERNMENT CODE 945.6. DEFENDANT'S CROSS-CLAIM FOR INVERSE CONDEMNATION FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW BECAUSE IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT TAKEN OR INTERFERED WITH DEFENDANT'S PROPERTY AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT SUBMITTED EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH THIS CLAIM. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED THAT DEFENDANT MUST VACATE THE PROPERTY AT PIER 38. DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM OCCUPYING THE CITY'S PROPERTY AT PIER 38 AND FROM MOORING HIS VESSEL AT PIER 38. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
ORDER ON STIPULATION FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE (SIGNED BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL/NO APPEARANCE BY DEFENSE)

Mtn For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ACTING BY AND THROUGH SAN FRANCISCO PORT COMMISSION'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS

Appearances:
Marc A. Centor of Cox Wootton Griffin Hansen & Poulos LLP (415.438.4600) appearing for plaintiff and moving party; No other appearances.

There being no other appearances, it is deemed that all other parties to this motion have stipulated to have the matter decided by the Judge Pro Tem. This matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions in the amount of $2040.00 is granted. The motion is unopposed. It should be noted that the papers submitted by the plaintiff demonstrate good cause for the granting of the motion. The City (plaintiff) previously obtained in August, 2013, an unopposed order to compel discovery from defendant with respect to earlier discovery requests from plaintiff that defendant had completely disregarded. Counsel for defendant at that time advised plaintiff that he had not been able to contact defendant to advise him of that order. The instant motion of plaintiff for the terminating and monetary sanctions is the result of defendant's complete disregard for, and non compliance with, the court's August order to compel discovery by defendant. Plaintiff's papers in support of its motion are persuasive. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Adrian Colley, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not reported (302/JPT)

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO PAUL CHARLTON AND CHALEUR, LLC's CROSS CLAIMS is Granted. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marc A. Centor, Esq. of Cox Wootton Griffin Hansen & Poulos, LLP, Tel. 415.418.4600, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was uncontested. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO ALL CLAIMS AND CROSS-CLAIMS BETWEEN THE CITY AND DEFENDANT CURTIS LIND is granted. No triable issue of material fact exists for any cause of action. Defendant and Cross-Complainant Lind does not dispute any of the facts recited in Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Disputed Facts. Plaintiff has prevailed on the trespass and nuisance claims as a matter of law. Defendant does not have a valid sublease for any portion of the property at Pier 38. Defendant is interfering with Plaintiff's free use and enjoyment of the property, which constitutes a nuisance under Code of Civil Procedure 3479. Defendant's cross-claims for breach of contract, intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, and negligent interference with prospective economic advantage are barred by the statute of limitations in California Government Code 945.6. Defendant's cross-claim for inverse condemnation fails as a matter of law because it is undisputed that plaintiff has not taken or interfered with defendant's property and defendant has not submitted evidence to establish this claim. It is further ordered that defendant must vacate the property at Pier 38. Defendant is enjoined from occupying the City's property at Pier 38 and from mooring his vessel at Pier 38. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-19-2012 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARC CENTOR, ESQ., COX WOOTTON GRIFFIN HANSEN & POULOS, 415.438.4600, FOR PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CHALEUR, LLC AND PAUL CHARLTON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff has standing to maintain this action under the SF Charter. Plaintiff's allegations that defendants have trespassed upon and misused Pier 38 are sufficient to support the claims. The existence of the unlawful detainer action is not a basis to dismiss or abate this action. The amended complaint is not uncertain and defendants can reasonably respond to the allegations. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
